chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يُدْرِكُ مَعَ الْإِمَامِ بَعْضَ صَلَاتِهِ وَ يُحْدِثُ الْإِمَامُ فَيُقَدِّمُهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 56 – A man attains part of his Salāt with the Imam, and the Imam breaks his ablution, so he asks him to lead

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/279)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/279)
text: قُلْتُ أَجِي‏ءُ إِلَى الْإِمَامِ وَ قَدْ سَبَقَنِي بِرَكْعَةٍ فِي الْفَجْرِ فَلَمَّا سَلَّمَ وَقَعَ فِي قَلْبِي أَنِّي أَتْمَمْتُ فَلَمْ أَزَلْ ذَاكِراً لِلَّهِ حَتَّى طَلَعَتِ الشَّمْسُ فَلَمَّا طَلَعَتْ نَهَضْتُ فَذَكَرْتُ أَنَّ الْإِمَامَ كَانَ سَبَقَنِي بِرَكْعَةٍ فَقَالَ إِنْ كُنْتَ فِي مَقَامِكَ فَأَتِمَّ بِرَكْعَةٍ وَ إِنْ كُنْتَ قَدِ انْصَرَفْتَ فَعَلَيْكَ الْإِعَادَةُ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Numan, from Al Husayn Bin Abu Al A’ala,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws, said, ‘I said, ‘I went over to the Imam and he was ahead of me by one Rak’at during Al-Fajr. So when he offered Salām, it occurred in my heart that I had completed. So I did not cease to mentioned Allahazwj until the sun emerged. So when the sun emerged, I got up and I remembered that the Imam had preceded me by one Rak’at’. So heasws said: ‘If you were in your place, so complete with one Rak’at, and if you had left, so upon you is the repeating’.
en.sarwar: 11. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali al-Nu‘man from al-Flusayn ibn abu al-‘Ala’ who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, ‘I come to the Imam when he is one Rak‘at ahead of me in the morning Salah (prayer). When he says Sal am (the phrase of offering greeting of peace) I, thinking to have completed, remain speaking of Allah until sunrise. At sunrise I stood up and remembered that the Imam was one Rak‘at ahead of me.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘If you are still in your place, complete it with one Rak‘at but if you have moved away, perform your Salah (prayer) again.’”
